comments.body: GitHub issue: https://github.com/navapbc/oscer/issues/680 (high-priority 500). Paired with #681 (placeholder label). Not yet started — raising with Jake/Jeff on Slack first since it sits on the in-flight OSCER-480 exemption-dashboard work (#641).

comments.author: Michael Giver
comments.body: Fix committed on giverm/OSCER-680-dashboard-500-automated-exemptions (dd0fe84d). TDD: red run reproduced the production NoMethodError + double-encoded payload; green after. Producer now stores an OSCER-idiomatic Hash ({ "exemption_reasons" => reason_codes }) instead of reasons.to_json; consumer resolves exemption type by decision_method (automated → reason code, manual → determination_data behind an is_a?(Hash) guard); added the missing veteran_disability_exempt locale key. RuboCop clean, full suite green. Not yet pushed / no PR — awaiting authorization.

comments.author: Michael Giver
comments.body: Closing: PR #683 (https://github.com/navapbc/oscer/pull/683, Resolves #680) is MERGED. Fix shipped — producer writes a Hash, consumer guards non-Hash determination_data, veteran_disability_exempt locale key added. Ticket was left stale in_progress; transitioning to done.

compiled_state.description:
# Summary

The member dashboard throws a 500 (`NoMethodError`) for any member whose certification has an **automated** exemption determination. Reproduced locally.

# Root cause

`determination_data` is a `jsonb` column (`db/schema.rb:339`), but `CertificationCase#record_exemption_determination` (`app/models/certification_case.rb:156`) writes `eligibility_fact.reasons.to_json` — a JSON **string** — into it. Postgres stores that as a string scalar, so ActiveRecord reads it back as a `String`, not a `Hash`. (The other `record_determination!` call sites write Hashes; `:156` is the only one calling `.to_json`.)

The member-dashboard consumer assumes a Hash with no guard:

```ruby
# app/models/member_dashboard_compliance.rb:294
def exemption_type_key_from_determination_data(data)
  return nil if data.blank?
  data.stringify_keys["exemption_type"].presence   # String has no #stringify_keys -> NoMethodError
end
```

`build_exemption_history` iterates every exempt determination, so a single String row 500s the page. The same Hash assumption exists at `app/models/determination.rb:115` (`ce_calculation_type`).

Confirmed on AWS dev: all 1024 exempt determinations read back as `String`; none carry an `exemption_type` key.

# Fix

1. **Consumer guard (stops the crash):** `exemption_type_key_from_determination_data` and `ce_calculation_type` return nil / skip when `determination_data` is not a Hash. Existing rows then fall through to the reasons-based fallback (`member_dashboard_compliance.rb:281`), which renders correct labels because automated exemption reason codes all end in `_exempt` (`age_under_19_exempt`, `veteran_disability_exempt`, etc.).
2. **Producer fix:** `certification_case.rb:156` writes `{}` instead of `eligibility_fact.reasons.to_json`. The reasons already live in the `reasons` column; no exemption-type payload is needed for automated exemptions.

# No backfill

No production environment exists, and the consumer guard + reasons-fallback already render the existing dev rows correctly. No data migration.

# Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Member dashboard renders (no 500) for a member with an automated exemption determination.
- [ ] Automated exemptions display the correct exemption label via the reasons fallback.
- [ ] `record_exemption_determination` writes a Hash (`{}`) to `determination_data`, not a JSON string.
- [ ] Specs cover: consumer guard against a non-Hash `determination_data`, and the producer writing a Hash.
compiled_state.title: Member dashboard 500s for automated exemption determinations
